annotations.label: Conditional probability (informal)

anchor.snippet: conditional probability is a measure of the probability of an event occurring, given that another event

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.cond μ s = (μ s)⁻¹ • μ.restrict s` is the standard formal counterpart of the informal lead definition.

annotations.label: Cough given sick

anchor.snippet: the probability that any given person has a cough on any given day may be only 5%

annotations.note: Informal illustrative example with no formal counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Independent events (via conditional probability)

anchor.snippet: then events A and B are said to be independent

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IndepSet` via the product rule `μ(s∩t)=μ(s)μ(t)` (`indepSet_iff_measure_inter_eq_mul`) rather than via the conditional-probability characterization.

annotations.label: Dengue fever test

anchor.snippet: if a person has dengue fever

annotations.note: Informal medical-test example, no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov definition of conditional probability

anchor.snippet: the conditional probability can be found by the quotient of the probability of the joint intersection

annotations.note: `cond_apply` gives `μ[t|s] = (μ s)⁻¹ * μ (s ∩ t)`, exactly Kolmogorov's quotient definition.

annotations.label: Conditional probability as an axiom (de Finetti)

anchor.snippet: prefer to introduce conditional probability as an axiom of probability

annotations.note: Mathlib derives conditional probability from a base measure rather than axiomatizing it à la de Finetti.

annotations.label: Goodman–Nguyen–Van Fraassen conditional event

anchor.section: As the probability of a conditional event
anchor.snippet: The Goodman–Nguyen–Van Fraassen conditional event can be defined as

annotations.note: No formalization of conditional events as nested infinite sequences in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional event probability meets Kolmogorov definition

anchor.section: As the probability of a conditional event
anchor.snippet: which meets the Kolmogorov definition of conditional probability

annotations.note: Depends on the Goodman–Nguyen–Van Fraassen construction, which is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditioning on probability-zero event is undefined

anchor.section: Conditioning on an event of probability zero
anchor.snippet: then according to the definition,

annotations.note: Mathlib sets `μ[|s] = 0` when `μ s = 0` (`cond_eq_zero`), reflecting that the conditional measure carries no meaningful information rather than being literally undefined.

annotations.label: Conditional density via limit (continuous case)

anchor.section: Conditioning on an event of probability zero
anchor.snippet: if two continuous random variables X and Y have a joint density

annotations.note: Mathlib's `condDistrib` provides a regular conditional distribution via disintegration on standard Borel spaces, not via the limit construction described.

annotations.label: Inconsistency of limit-based conditional probability

anchor.section: Conditioning on an event of probability zero
anchor.snippet: but this cannot be done in a consistent manner

annotations.note: Meta-statement about non-existence of a coherent limit-based definition; not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borel–Kolmogorov paradox

anchor.section: Conditioning on an event of probability zero
anchor.snippet: The Borel–Kolmogorov paradox demonstrates this with a geometrical argument

annotations.note: The geometric paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional probability given a discrete random variable

anchor.section: Conditioning on a discrete random variable
anchor.snippet: For a value x in V and an event A , the conditional probability is given by

annotations.note: Expressible as `μ[A | X ⁻¹' {x}]` using `cond` applied to a fiber (as in `sum_meas_smul_cond_fiber`), but there is no dedicated definition wrapping it.

annotations.label: Expected value of conditional probability equals unconditional

anchor.section: Conditioning on a discrete random variable
anchor.snippet: From the law of total probability , its expected value is equal to the unconditional probability of A

annotations.note: `sum_meas_smul_cond_fiber` states the law of total probability over fibers of a finite-valued random variable.

annotations.label: Partial conditional probability

anchor.snippet: is about the probability of event

annotations.note: Partial conditional probabilities (à la Draheim) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jeffrey conditionalization

anchor.snippet: Jeffrey conditionalization

annotations.note: Jeffrey's rule of updating on uncertain evidence has no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Two dice conditional probability

anchor.snippet: Suppose that somebody secretly rolls two fair six-sided dice

annotations.note: Worked dice example not formalized.

annotations.label: Conditional probability as update of probability

anchor.snippet: the conditional probability is an update of the probability of an event based on new information

annotations.note: Philosophical/epistemic characterization; Mathlib's `cond` is the measure-theoretic object without an explicit update-interpretation lemma.

annotations.label: Morse code transmission errors

anchor.snippet: When Morse code is transmitted, there is a certain probability that the

annotations.note: Channel-coding example not formalized.

annotations.label: Statistical independence of events

anchor.snippet: Events A and B are defined to be statistically independent if the probability of the intersection of A and B is equal to the product

annotations.note: `IndepSet` together with `indepSet_iff_measure_inter_eq_mul` gives the product-measure characterization of independence.

annotations.label: Conditional independence

anchor.snippet: the pair is defined to be conditionally independent

annotations.note: `CondIndepSet` (and companions `CondIndep`, `CondIndepFun`) formalize conditional independence.

annotations.label: Mutually exclusive events are not independent

anchor.snippet: mutually exclusive events cannot be statistically independent

annotations.note: No direct lemma found stating that disjoint nontrivial events are never independent.

annotations.label: Relating P(A|B) and P(B|A) via Bayes' theorem

anchor.section: Assuming conditional probability is of similar size to its inverse
anchor.snippet: The relationship between P ( A | B ) and P ( B | A ) is given by Bayes' theorem

annotations.note: `cond_eq_inv_mul_cond_mul` is Bayes' theorem: `μ[t|s] = (μ s)⁻¹ * μ[s|t] * μ t`.

annotations.label: Marginal and conditional linked by law of total probability

anchor.section: Assuming marginal and conditional probabilities are of similar size
anchor.snippet: These probabilities are linked through the law of total probability

annotations.note: `cond_add_cond_compl_eq` states `μ[t|s]·μ s + μ[t|sᶜ]·μ sᶜ = μ t`, the two-set law of total probability.

annotations.label: Selection bias in medical claim

anchor.section: Assuming marginal and conditional probabilities are of similar size
anchor.snippet: in the context of a medical claim

annotations.note: Informal example illustrating base-rate confusion; no formal counterpart.

annotations.label: Formal derivation of conditional probability

anchor.snippet: Formally, P ( A | B ) is defined as the probability of A according to a new probability function on the sample space

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.cond` realizes this construction as `(μ s)⁻¹ • μ.restrict s`, a probability measure when `μ s` is finite and nonzero (`cond_isProbabilityMeasure`).
